name: Dexo - trail along the North Coast of Oleiros
name_original: Dexo – sendero por Costa Norte de Oleiros
region_intro: Hiking in Costa De Dexo offers access to a distinctive 11-kilometer stretch of coastline in Oleiros, Galicia. This Natural Monument is characterized by exposed, heavily eroded cliffs composed of crystalline schists and metamorphic rocks, forming a serrated coastline with embedded beaches and unique marine caves known as "furnas." The terrestrial landscape behind the cliffs features grasslands, heather, and gorse. These easy hiking trails provide opportunities to explore the rugged coastal environment and its natural features. Best easy hiking trails in Costa De Dexo The most popular easy hiking route is Seixo Branco Caves loop from ZEC Costa de Dexo, a 4.4 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This route explores the coastal path near the notable "O Seixo Branco" quartz vein. Another top favourite among local hikers is Dexo Marina Dolphin Monument – Seixo Branco Caves loop from ZEC Costa de Dexo, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This trail offers views of the coastline and passes by the Dexo Marina Dolphin Monument. Local hikers also love the Seixo Branco Caves loop from Illa do Portelo, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes and near the marine caves, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Hiking in Costa De Dexo is defined by exposed coastlines, eroded cliffs, and unique marine caves, with easy hiking trails offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Costa De Dexo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 400 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Costa De Dexo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Costa De Dexo?
A: Costa De Dexo offers a wide selection of easy hiking trails, with over 100 routes specifically graded as easy. In total, there are nearly 200 hiking trails to explore in the region, catering to various preferences.

Q: Are there family-friendly easy trails in Costa De Dexo?
A: Yes, the easy hiking trails in Costa De Dexo are generally suitable for families. Many routes follow the coastline, offering gentle paths and stunning views of the Atlantic, making them enjoyable for all ages. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10503305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seixo Branco Caves loop from ZEC Costa de Dexo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy option.

Q: Can I bring my dog on easy hikes in Costa De Dexo?
A: While the region is known for its natural beauty, specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife, especially marine birds, and to ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always check local signage for any restrictions.

Q: Are there circular easy hiking routes in Costa De Dexo?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails in Costa De Dexo are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10503305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seixo Branco Caves loop from ZEC Costa de Dexo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38204027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dexo Marina Dolphin Monument – Seixo Branco Caves loop from ZEC Costa de Dexo&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic loops along the coast.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see on easy hikes?
A: Easy hikes in Costa De Dexo allow you to discover unique natural features and landmarks. You can explore the dramatic, eroded cliffs, embedded beaches, and marine caves known as 'furnas'. A prominent landmark is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3850169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The White Pebble&lt;/a&gt; (O Seixo Branco), a striking white quartz vein. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dexo Marina Dolphin Monument&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Thief's Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for easy trails in Costa De Dexo?
A: Parking is generally available at common trailheads and points of interest along the Costa De Dexo. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10503305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seixo Branco Caves loop from ZEC Costa de Dexo&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find parking near the ZEC Costa de Dexo area. It's advisable to check specific starting points for designated parking areas.

Q: Is public transport available to access easy hiking trails?
A: While Costa De Dexo is accessible, public transport options directly to all trailheads might be limited. It's best to research local bus routes connecting to towns like Oleiros or Mera, and then plan your hike from there, or consider a short taxi ride to reach specific starting points.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hiking in Costa De Dexo?
A: The best time for easy hiking in Costa De Dexo is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is milder and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, but it's wise to start early to avoid the midday sun. Winter offers a wilder, more dramatic coastal experience, but be prepared for potentially strong winds and rain.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I spot on easy trails?
A: Costa De Dexo is a significant habitat for marine birds, making it excellent for birdwatching. You might spot European shags, Caspian gulls, peregrine falcons, and common kestrels. The terrestrial areas behind the cliffs also host various plants and insects. Keep an eye out for the diverse flora and fauna that thrive in this protected coastal environment.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about easy trails in Costa De Dexo?
A: The easy trails in Costa De Dexo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 500 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the breathtaking views of the wild Atlantic coast, the unique geological formations like the 'furnas' and 'O Seixo Branco', and the opportunity to connect with a well-preserved coastal environment. The accessibility and variety of easy routes are also often highlighted.

Q: Are there viewpoints along the easy trails?
A: Absolutely. Many easy trails in Costa De Dexo offer magnificent viewpoints. The Mera lighthouses provide a superb vantage point over the Atlantic horizon, where the estuaries of A Coruña, Betanzos, and Ares converge. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3850169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The White Pebble&lt;/a&gt; (O Seixo Branco) also offers impressive views of the rugged coastline and even the city of A Coruña in the distance.

Q: Are there any historical sites along the easy trails?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, Costa De Dexo holds historical significance. Along the trails, you can find ancient military ruins and four 'castros' (hill forts) scattered across the landscape, offering a glimpse into the region's past. These sites add an interesting cultural dimension to your easy coastal hike.
tips: The Dexo Marina in A Coruña is marked by a circular monument with dolphins, highlighting the wildlife in this natural park ecosystem
The stone formation is believed to have been used by pirates who used the inlets of the North Coast for hiding and refuge from the navy.
Nice fairytale like walk. Muddy if bad weather
